deep_dive.executive_summary:
At about $372.46 per share, the base annual return is about 1.44% per year over seven years (total synthesis).

Analog Devices is a scaled high-performance analog semiconductor franchise spanning industrial automation, automotive electrification, communications infrastructure, and emerging data-center power delivery. FY2025 results show strong cash conversion: $8.61 of free cash flow per diluted share on $11.0 billion of revenue, with Industrial at 45% and Automotive at 30% of end-market mix. The business is understandable as a pricing-power compounder, but leverage from the Maxim deal and ...

pricing_analysis.conclusion: Entry prices were computed mechanically from the routed power-zone profile (High-return compounder). They are decision inputs, not a decision; the owner must review the scenarios before acting.
pricing_analysis.strongest_counter_explanation: growth projected without its capital cost
pricing_analysis.economic_claim_note: Per-share claims use fully diluted shares from valuation.inputs.

trigger.policy: A complete economic-value analysis is required when any trigger fires; otherwise it is recommended.

economic_claim.description: One diluted share of ADI, including analog semiconductor owner cash, AI/data-center edge option, net financial claims, and cycle/integration reserve.
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: Core owner free cash flow is valued once; AI/data-center option, net financial claims, and cycle reserve are separate additive components with unique overlap keys.

economic_value_bridge.accounting_reference: FY2025 10-K and Q2 FY2026 10-Q filing extracts; GAAP net income is cross-check only for analog compounder.

component_groups.label: Analog semiconductor owner-cash engine
component_groups.adjustments: Reconcile to FY2025 10-K and Q2 FY2026 10-Q before decision use.

component_groups.label: AI and data-center power and connectivity option
component_groups.economic_claim: AI and data-center power and connectivity option
component_groups.adjustments: Reconcile to FY2025 10-K and Q2 FY2026 10-Q before decision use.

risk_and_timing.timing_basis: Data-center power delivery and high-speed connectivity design wins over 3 to 7 years.
risk_and_timing.probability_basis: Judgment from FY2025 Industrial revenue $4.93B and 10-K data-center market commentary; ADI does not disclose standalone AI revenue.
risk_and_timing.remaining_capital_basis: Incremental R&D embedded in consolidated $1.77B FY2025 spend.

limitations: AI/data-center milestone value is judgment-based; industrial revenue partially embedded in consolidated free cash flow.
Cycle and integration reserve bands remain widest judgment components.

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_provenance.label: Owner earnings and reinvestment-return DCF
method_provenance.equation: cash_t = cash_(t-1)*(1+reinvestment*incremental_ROIC); value = PV(distributable cash + terminal owner earnings)

high.rationale: Non-overlapping claim on hyperscale power delivery, thermal management, and high-speed connectivity beyond normalized free cash flow; base is judgment band.

high.label: AI and data-center edge option per share

source_lineage.locator: Disaggregated revenue: Industrial $4,929.4M (45% of FY2025 revenue)

options.evidence: Primary bridge from ADI/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20251125_rpt20251101_acc0000006281_25_000153.htm: FY2025 revenue $11019.7M, OCF $4812.2M, FCF $4278.6M, Industrial end market $4,929.4M, cash $2499.4M, total debt $8599.9M; contract backfill 2026-07-21.

probability_and_timing.timing_basis: Data-center power delivery and high-speed connectivity design wins over 3 to 7 years.
probability_and_timing.probability_basis: Judgment from FY2025 Industrial revenue $4.93B and 10-K data-center market commentary; ADI does not disclose standalone AI revenue.
probability_and_timing.remaining_capital_basis: Incremental R&D embedded in consolidated $1.77B FY2025 spend.

options.tax_and_realization_adjustments: Reconcile to FY2025 10-K and Q2 FY2026 10-Q before decision use.
options.falsifier: Primary evidence shows claim, cash conversion, or capital structure is materially worse than low case.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: High-return compounder
applicability_tests: Owner earnings can be separated from stock compensation, working capital, and maintenance capital.
Incremental returns on retained capital are observable and durable.
The competitive advantage explains pricing, retention, share, or cost outcomes.
Terminal value is supported by a reinvestment runway rather than an unexplained multiple.
failure_modes: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: Component changes are classified from observable differences in quantity, comparable method, probability, timing, capital, evidence, and schedule scope.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

insight_events.title: Operating income accelerating
insight_events.summary: Operating income growth moved from +102.9% to +103.5% YoY for two consecutive quarters. Trailing-twelve-month growth confirms the same direction. Source: SEC XBRL quarterly filings.
insight_events.why_it_matters: Positive evidence. May change earnings power, cash generation, or the valuation range.
insight_events.recommended_follow_up: Compare this development with the thesis, falsifiers, and valuation assumptions.

insight_events.summary: Growth regime upshift: YoY growth moved to +66.5% to +103.5% YoY (baseline median +13.5% YoY) for two consecutive quarters. Source: SEC XBRL quarterly filings.

timeline.label: Q2 FY2026 10-Q: six-month revenue $6.78B, net income $2.01B; FCF run-rate ~$8/sh annualized
FY2025 10-K: revenue $11.0B (+17% YoY), OCF $4.81B, FCF $4.28B ($8.61/sh); Industrial 45% of revenue
FY2024 trough year: revenue $9.43B, net income $1.64B as industrial and communications softened
FY2023 peak cycle: revenue $12.31B, net income $3.31B before broad semiconductor correction
Maxim Integrated acquisition closes, doubling analog scale and adding automotive/industrial mix
Shareholders approve Maxim Integrated acquisition; leverage rises to fund analog consolidation
Analog Devices announces agreement to acquire Maxim Integrated Products
Linear Technology acquisition closes, expanding power management and industrial analog portfolio
Company announces agreement to acquire Linear Technology for ~$14.8B
Analog Devices founded in Norwood, Massachusetts; decades-long analog and mixed-signal franchise

industry.structure: Oligopolistic high-performance analog and mixed-signal semiconductors: ADI, Texas Instruments, and NXP compete on design breadth, manufacturing scale, and customer intimacy across industrial, automotive, and communications end markets.
industry.share_shift: ADI gained scale through Linear (2017) and Maxim (2021) integrations; FY2025 revenue recovered to $11.0B with Industrial at 45% and Automotive at 30% of mix.
industry.trend: Electrification, factory automation, and AI data-center power delivery expand analog content per system; cyclical inventory corrections remain the primary near-term swing factor.
